repo_prior_art.cite: none; no in-catalogue Stat entry concerns this two-period g-formula representation.

published_prior_art.cite: LuedtkeSofryginVanderLaanCarone2017 gives longitudinal g-formula/backward-regression representations and sequential double robustness.
This is the standard bounded-linear-integral consequence of L1-valued Hölder regularity of the transition kernel; no distinct causal result is claimed.

repo_prior_art.cite: In-catalogue Stat proposals concern policy regret, dose response, and DP CATE, not a transition-composed longitudinal g-formula minimax frontier.

published_prior_art.cite: CinelliFellerImbensKennedyMagliacaneZubizarreta2025 identifies non-root-n time-varying-treatment optimality as open; LuedtkeSofryginVanderLaanCarone2017 is first-order SDR; RobinsLiTchetgenVanderVaart2008 and RobinsLiMukherjeeTchetgenVanderVaart2017 provide general/single-time HOIF machinery rather than this matched composed frontier.
RobinsTchetgenLiVanderVaart2009 establishes the two-convex-mixture device for multiparameter semiparametric functionals, not the proposed normalized longitudinal transition construction.

LuedtkeSofryginVanderLaanCarone2017 analyzes first-order longitudinal remainders, while RobinsLiMukherjeeTchetgenVanderVaart2017 gives general higher-order estimating equations, neither a minimal-order frontier for recursively fitted pseudo-outcomes.
RobinsLiTchetgenVanderVaart2008 supplies the single-time HOIF slow-rate benchmark recovered after removal of the first transition channel.

novelty_flags.label: related_work / checklist entry RobinsLiTchetgenVanderVaart2016
novelty_flags.one_line: The cited SPA quadratic-estimator paper illustrates density/regression-square and missing-response estimation, not time-dependent treatment with time-varying confounding; that latter claim belongs to the distinct 2016 HOIF technical report (arXiv:1601.05820).

novelty_flags.label: oeq:frontier
novelty_flags.one_line: The flagship frontier invokes Cinelli et al. and the HOIF literature without a section, page, equation, or quoted open-problem anchor; add Cinelli et al. §3.5 and precise locations in the comparator papers.

novelty_flags.label: oeq:chained-converse
novelty_flags.one_line: The two-convex-mixture comparison cites RobinsTchetgenLiVanderVaart2009 without an anchor to its mixture construction or lower-bound theorem.

soundness_flags.label: def:chained-fuzzy-mixtures / oeq:chained-converse
soundness_flags.one_line: The displayed law specifies only P(Y=1|·) although Y has support [-1,1], leaving the rest of the conditional outcome law and hence m2 and the asserted 2ρεδ target separation undefined; explicitly make Y Rademacher (or specify the residual law) and recalculate the separation.

soundness_flags.label: oeq:frontier
soundness_flags.one_line: The risk display uses \widehat\psi_n^{\mathrm{SH}} without selecting or quantifying correction orders (q1,q2), so it is not a single estimator; state an existential/order-selection rule tied to \operatorname{Min}(\mathcal Q).

accepted_scope_caveats.label: oeq:frontier
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Known smoothness indices, strict overlap, iid sampling, exact two-period state sufficiency, and the fixed horizon are treated as conditional regime restrictions.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Any matched rate/elbow claim is conditional on this fixed-index, exactly state-sufficient two-period Markov class; adaptation, approximate sufficiency, and longer horizons are excluded.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Complete the finite-mixture outcome law, preferably with Y∈{-1,1}, and write the resulting m2, ψ(Pz,λ,μ), and exact ± mixture separation before stating the Hellinger calculation.

Turn the upper question into a precise existential construction: give a measurable order-selection rule or quantify a pair (q1,q2)∈Min(Q), then state the conditional bias and RMSE bound for that estimator.

Replace the misattributed 2016 SPA comparator with the appropriate HOIF technical report if it is needed, and add location anchors for Cinelli §3.5 and the relevant two-mixture result in RobinsTchetgenLiVanderVaart2009.

Give explicit resolution/amplitude schedules for separate r1 and r2 lower-bound branches and show how each preserves the stated Hölder balls and positivity while retaining bounded mixture Hellinger distance.

recommended_next_step: Revise the incomplete lower-bound outcome law and the unspecified upper-estimator order selection, then repair the comparator attribution and anchors before re-reviewing the matched frontier.
